# terrible_community_general_oneandone_private_network (Resource)





<!-- schema generated by tfplugindocs -->
## Schema

### Required

- `host_id` (String) ID of the `terrible_host` to run this task against

### Optional

- `add_members` (String) List of server identifiers (name or id) to be added to the private network.
- `api_url` (String) Custom API URL. Overrides the `ONEANDONE_API_URL` environment variable.
- `async_seconds` (Number) Run the task asynchronously, timing out after this many seconds. 0 = synchronous (default).
- `auth_token` (String) Authenticating API token provided by 1&1.
- `changed_when` (String) Jinja2 expression that overrides when the task is considered changed (e.g. 'false').
- `datacenter` (String) The identifier of the datacenter where the private network is created.
- `delegate_to_id` (String) ID of another terrible_host to delegate execution to.
- `description` (String) Set a description for the network.
- `environment` (String) Environment variables set for the task (dict of nameâ†’value).
- `failed_when` (String) Jinja2 expression that overrides when the task is considered failed.
- `ignore_errors` (Boolean) When true, a failed task does not raise a Terraform error.
- `name` (String) Private network name used with present state. Used as identifier (id or name) when used with absent state.
- `network_address` (String) Set a private network space, for example `192.168.1.0`.
- `poll_interval` (Number) Polling interval in seconds when async_seconds > 0. Defaults to 15.
- `private_network` (String) The identifier (id or name) of the network used with update state.
- `remove_members` (String) List of server identifiers (name or id) to be removed from the private network.
- `skip_tags` (String) Skip tasks with these Ansible tags (list of strings).
- `state` (String) Define a network's state to create, remove, or update.
- `subnet_mask` (String) Set the netmask for the private network, for example `255.255.255.0`.
- `tags` (String) Run only tasks with these Ansible tags (list of strings).
- `timeout` (Number) Override the default execution timeout (seconds). Defaults to 300.
- `triggers` (String) Arbitrary map of values; any change triggers task re-execution
- `wait` (Boolean) Wait for the instance to be in state 'running' before returning.
- `wait_interval` (Number) Defines the number of seconds to wait when using the _wait_for methods.
- `wait_timeout` (Number) How long before wait gives up, in seconds.

### Read-Only

- `changed` (Boolean) Whether the task reported a change
- `id` (String) Unique task resource ID
